title: Stroller and seat height adjusting mechanism thereof
abstract: The present invention discloses a seat height adjusting mechanism (30) disposed on a stroller frame (10) of a stroller (100a). The seat height adjusting mechanism (30) includes a holding base (31). The holding base (31) is assembled with a seat (20) and is slidable upward and downward along the stroller frame (10) for adjusting a height of the seat (20) mounted on the holding base (31) relative to the stroller frame (10). The seat height adjusting mechanism (30) of the present invention can utilize upward and downward sliding of the holding base (31) relative to the stroller frame (10) to adjust the height of the seat (20) mounted on the holding base (31) relative to the stroller frame (10) for infants with different heights. In addition, the present invention further discloses a stroller (100a) including the seat height adjusting mechanism (30).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ, 40) applied to being disposed on a stroller frame (10) of a stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOe, lOOf), the seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ, 40) characterized by: a holding base (31, 41) assembled with a seat (20) and slidable upward and downward along the stroller frame (10), for adjusting a height of the seat (20) mounted on the holding base (31, 41) relative to the stroller frame (10). 2. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) of claim 1, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) further comprises a locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), a release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) linked with the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and a fixing member (34) mounted on the stroller frame (10), the holding base (31) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34), the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is mounted on one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) and at least has a locking position and a releasing position, the other of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) has at least two engaging portions (35) spaced apart from each other along a sliding direction of the holding base (31), the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is located at the locking position when the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is selectively engaged with any of the two engaging portions (35), and the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) moves the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) to be disengaged from any of the two engaging portions (35) for making the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) located at the releasing position. 3. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) of claim 1, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises at least two locking members (32) spaced apart from each other along a sliding direction of the holding base (31), a release actuator (33) linked with the at least two locking members (32), and a fixing member (34) mounted on the stroller frame (10), the holding base (31) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34), the at least two locking members (32) are mounted on one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) and at least have a locking position and a releasing position, the other of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) has an 

 engaging portion (35), any of the at least two locking members (32) is located at the locking position when selectively engaged with the engaging portion (35), and any of the at least two locking members (32) is disengaged from the engaging portion (35) and is located at the releasing position when the release actuator (33) moves the at least two locking members (32). 4. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) further comprises an elastic member (36, 36ГўВҖВҷ) disposed on the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) having the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted thereon, and the elastic member (36, 36ГўВҖВҷ) has a tendency to drive the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) from the releasing position to the locking position. 5. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) of claim 4, characterized in that at least one of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the engaging portion (35) has an inclined structure (321, 351) oblique to the sliding direction of the holding base (31), and the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) moves from the locking position to the releasing position along the inclined structure (321, 351) during upward sliding of the holding base (31). 6. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) of claim 5, characterized in that the inclined structure (321) of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted on the holding base (31) is located at an upper side of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and the inclined structure (351) of the engaging portion (35) on the fixing member (34) is located at an upper side of the engaging portion (35); or, the inclined structure (321) of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted on the fixing member (34) is located at a lower side of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and the inclined structure (351) of the engaging portion (35) on the holding base (31) is located at a lower side of the engaging portion (35). 7. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is slidable, and a sliding 

 direction of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) intersects with the sliding direction of the holding base (31). 8. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30) of claim 7, characterized in that the release actuator (33) is formed integrally with the locking member (32). 9. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 7, characterized in that the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) is independent from the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ). 10. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 9, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) further comprises a pulling member (37) having flexibility, and the release actuator (33) is linked with the locking member (32) via the pulling member (37). 11. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) of claim 10, characterized in that a first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the release actuator (33) mounted on the stroller frame (10), and a second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the locking member (32) mounted on the fixing member (34). 12. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) of claim 11, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises a rotating plate (38) pivoted to the stroller frame (10), the rotating plate (38) has a first mounting portion (381) and a second mounting portion (382) located at two sides of a central axis of the rotating plate (38) respectively, the release actuator (33) is connected to the first mounting portion (381), and the first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the second mounting portion (382). 13. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) of claim 12, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises a pushing rod (39a), the pushing rod (39a) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34) or the holding base (31) along the sliding direction of the holding base (31), the second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the pushing rod (39a), and the pushing rod (39a) is linked with the locking member (32). 14. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 10, characterized in that a first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the release actuator (33) mounted on the seat (20), and a second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the locking member (32) mounted on the holding base (31). 15. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 14, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) further comprises a transmission member (39b) movably disposed on the seat (20), the transmission member (39b) is linked with the release actuator (33), and the first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the transmission member (39b). 16. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 15, characterized in that the release actuator (33) and the transmission member (39b) are slidably disposed on the seat (20) respectively, and a sliding direction of the release actuator (33) intersects with a sliding direction of the transmission member (39b). 17. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that the locking member (32) is pivotable, and the release actuator (33) is formed integrally with the locking member (32). 18. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) are pivotably mounted on the holding base (31) or the fixing member (34) for releasing the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) via pivoting of the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ). 19. The seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) of claim 2 or 3, characterized in that the engaging portion (35) is a slot structure. 20. The seat height adjusting mechanism (40) of claim 1, characterized in that the holding base (41) is pivoted to the stroller frame (10) about a central rotating axis of the holding base (41), the holding base (41) at least has a first assembly 

 head (41a) and a second assembly head (41b) spaced apart from each other along a rotating direction of the holding base (41), a distance between the first assembly head (41a) and the central rotating axis is different from a distance between the second assembly head (41b) and the central rotating axis, and the seat (20) is detachably mounted on the first assembly head (41a) or the second assembly head (41b). 21. The seat height adjusting mechanism (40) of claim 20, characterized in that a central angle between the first assembly head (41a) and the second assembly head (41b) is 90ГӮВ°. 22. A stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOe, lOOf) comprising: a stroller frame (10) comprising a front leg (11), a rear leg (12), and a handle (13) foldable relative to each other; a seat (20); and characterized by: a seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ, 40) comprising: a holding base (31, 41) assembled with the seat (20) and slidable upward and downward along the stroller frame (10), for adjusting a height of the seat (20) mounted on the holding base (31, 41) relative to the stroller frame (10). 23. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOf) of claim 22, characterized in that the holding base (31) is slidable upward and downward along the handle (13), the front leg (11), or the rear leg (12). 24. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOf ) of claim 22, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) further comprises a locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), a release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) linked with the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and a fixing member (34) mounted on the stroller frame (10), the holding base (31) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34), the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is mounted on one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) and at least has a locking position and a releasing position, the other of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) has at least two engaging portions (35) spaced apart from each other along a sliding direction of the holding base (31), 

 the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is located at the locking position when the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is selectively engaged with any of the two engaging portions (35), and the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) moves the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) to be disengaged from any of the two engaging portions (35) for making the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) located at the releasing position. 25. The stroller (lOOd) of claim 22, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises at least two locking members (32) spaced apart from each other along a sliding direction of the holding base (31), a release actuator (33) linked with the at least two locking members (32), and a fixing member (34) mounted on the stroller frame (10), the holding base (31) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34), the at least two locking members (32) are mounted on one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) and at least have a locking position and a releasing position, the other of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) has an engaging portion (35), any of the at least two locking members (32) is located at the locking position when selectively engaged with the engaging portion (35), and any of the at least two locking members (32) is disengaged from the engaging portion (35) and is located at the releasing position when the release actuator (33) moves the at least two locking members (32). 26. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOf) of claim 24 or 25, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30, 30ГўВҖВҷ, 30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВқГўВҖВҷ) further comprises an elastic member (36, 36ГўВҖВҷ) disposed on the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the one of the fixing member (34) and the holding base (31) having the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted thereon, and the elastic member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) has a tendency to drive the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) from the releasing position to the locking position. 27. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOf) of claim 26, characterized in that at least one of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the engaging portion (35) has an inclined structure (321, 351) oblique to the sliding direction of the holding base (31), and the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) moves from the locking position to the 

 releasing position along the inclined structure (321, 351) during upward sliding of the holding base (31). 28. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOf) of claim 27, characterized in that the inclined structure (321) of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted on the holding base (31) is located at an upper side of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and the inclined structure (351) of the engaging portion (35) on the fixing member (34) is located at an upper side of the engaging portion (35); or, the inclined structure (321) of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) mounted on the fixing member (34) is located at a lower side of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ), and the inclined structure (351) of the engaging portion (35) on the holding base (31) is located at a lower side of the engaging portion (35). 29. The stroller (100a, 100b, 100c, lOOd, lOOf) of claim 24 or 25, characterized in that the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) is slidable, and a sliding direction of the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) intersects with the sliding direction of the holding base (31). 30. The stroller (100a, 100b) of claim 29, characterized in that the release actuator (33) is formed integrally with the locking member (32), and the seat (20) is detachably assembled with the holding base (31). 31. The stroller (lOOd, lOOf) of claim 29, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ, 30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) further comprises a pulling member (37) having flexibility, the release actuator (33) and the locking member (32) are independent from each other, and the release actuator (33) is linked with the locking member (32) via the pulling member (37). 32. The stroller (lOOd) of claim 31, characterized in that a first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the release actuator (33) mounted on the handle (13), and a second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the locking member (32) mounted on the fixing member (34). 33. The stroller (lOOd) of claim 32, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises a rotating plate (38) pivoted to the handle (13), the rotating plate (38) has a first mounting portion (381) and a second mounting portion (382) located at two sides of a central axis of the rotating plate (38) respectively, the release actuator (33) is connected to the first mounting portion (381), the first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the second mounting portion (382), and the seat (20) is detachably assembled with the holding base (31). 34. The stroller (lOOd) of claim 33, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВқ) further comprises a pushing rod (39a), the pushing rod (39a) is slidably disposed on the fixing member (34) or the holding base (31) along the sliding direction of the holding base (31), the second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the pushing rod (39a), and the pushing rod (39a) is linked with the locking member (32). 35. The stroller (lOOf) of claim 31, characterized in that the seat height adjusting mechanism (30ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ) further comprises a transmission member (39b) movably disposed on the seat (20), the transmission member (39b) is linked with the release actuator (33), the first end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the transmission member (39b), and the second end of the pulling member (37) is connected to the locking member (32) mounted on the holding base (31). 36. The stroller (lOOf) of claim 35, characterized in that the release actuator (33) and the transmission member (39b) are slidably disposed on the seat (20) respectively, and a sliding direction of the release actuator (33) intersects with a sliding direction of the transmission member (39b). 37. The stroller (100a, 100b) of claim 24 or 25, characterized in that the locking member (32) is pivotable, and the release actuator (33) is formed integrally with the locking member (32). 38. The stroller (100c, lOOd) of claim 24 or 25, characterized in that the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) and the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ) are pivotably mounted on 

 the holding base (31) or the fixing member (34) for releasing the locking member (32, 32ГўВҖВҷ) via pivoting of the release actuator (33, 33ГўВҖВҷ). 39. The stroller (lOOe) of claim 22, characterized in that the holding base (41) is pivoted to the stroller frame (10) about a central rotating axis of the holding base (41), the holding base (41) at least has a first assembly head (41a) and a second assembly head (41b) spaced apart from each other along a rotating direction of the holding base (41), a distance between the first assembly head (41a) and the central rotating axis is different from a distance between the second assembly head (41b) and the central rotating axis, and the seat (20) is detachably mounted on the first assembly head (41a) or the second assembly head (41b).
